title: Smoothed Stochastic Momentum Index [Mr. Quant]
thesis_bullets: TradingView indicator: Smoothed Stochastic Momentum Index [Mr.

From Live stream on March 3rd 2026 The Smoothed Stochastic Momentum Index (SSMI) is a high-precision scalping tool designed to filter out market noise and align fast-paced momentum entries with broader market structure.
Here is a breakdown of how the logic works from the core "engine" to the final entry signals: 1.
The Core Engine: Double Smoothing Unlike a standard Stochastic oscillator that can be very "choppy," the SSMI uses a Double Smoothed Stochastic (DSS) calculation.

strategy_heuristics_used: Every hypothesis should include explicit regime assumptions and invalidate conditions.
Separate signal logic from execution logic to reduce confounded performance attribution.
Require risk gating concepts before scaling complexity in entry or exit rules.
automation_heuristics_used: Keep human approval checkpoints for high-impact automation and deployment transitions.
Enforce deterministic session gating before any live-capable execution pathway is enabled.
Invest first in telemetry for risk, latency, and execution drift before adding new automation features.
